primary_method: component liquidation-basis NAV with dated payoff curve
weighting_rationale: GYRO reports on ASC 205-30 liquidation-basis accounting, so the economic claim is audited net assets in liquidation per share, not recurring owner earnings. The routed primary method (component_owner_cash_and_unit_nav) maps real-estate NRV, cash, senior debt, and the net liquidation-cost accrual once, with the B2K entitlement embedded in real_estate_nrv rather than double-counted. That dated-payoff path dominates because it matches the legal wind-down structure and ties directly to quarterly filing estimates. Comparable-transaction and replacement-cost corroborators stay silent in practice: no indexed like-for-like Long Island or Westchester land sales were available at packet freeze. Marks credit-cycle review is a senior-claim survivability test, not an alternate equity value; it bounds financing tail risk but does not replace the liquidation NAV bridge. The chair does not average the headline 33% base return, the 13.5% entitlement-slippage bear, and the ~3% component-low credit stub into a blended consensus.
agreed_facts: As of 2026-03-31, audited liquidation-basis net assets were $11.79 per share on 2,199,308 shares outstanding, versus a market price near $5.80 (roughly 51% discount).
Real estate is carried at management estimated net realizable value on liquidation-basis accounting; the disclosed NAV is already a fair-value floor, not a GAAP historical-cost understatement.

The signed B2K purchase agreement (2025-07-30) prices the ~49-acre Flowerfield parcel at $24.0M to $28.74M gross; the base real-estate NRV already uses the $28.74M high end before Smithtown site-plan approval.

Net assets in liquidation per share fell from $13.91 (12/31/2024) to $11.76 (12/31/2025), about 15%, before ticking to $11.79 in Q1 2026; management has extended the target dissolution date twice and now targets 2028-12-31.

Cash was $3,950,227 against loans payable of $10,790,194 as of 2026-03-31; three disclosed facility groups (bank line, 2021 Rialto mortgage, 2023 LLYR mortgage) are named but do not fully reconcile to the balance-sheet total in the frozen extract.
The Article 78 appeal on the Flowerfield eight-lot subdivision and B2K site-plan approval with the Town of Smithtown remained pending as of the 10-Q filed 2026-05-13.
All three independent raters voted watch in round two; no rater recommended add, buy, hold-for-sizing, or pass.
disputed_facts: Whether the ~51% market discount is mispricing or fair compensation for re-estimatable management NAV, micro-cap illiquidity (~2,000-6,000 shares/day), and unresolved entitlement litigation.

Whether the October 2026 Rialto mortgage maturity extends, refinances acceptably, or forces punitive repricing or asset sales ahead of the B2K closing clock (credit cross-check flagged insufficient_evidence).
Per-share impact of a dilutive capital raise through 2028: management discloses possible additional capital needs, but the frozen valuation path does not quantify issuance at the current share count.
Whether the B2K high-end mark and 2028 timeline survive Article 78 and Smithtown approval without another NAV downward re-estimation.
Whether scarce-land or catalyst-asset framing adds explanatory power beyond a finite liquidation schedule (HK dissent: this is a wind-down, not a durable recurring monetization asset).
strongest_dissent: Marks credit-cycle cross-check: even with real collateral, a failed or punitive October 2026 Rialto refinance on top of the existing 15% LLYR facility could force asset sales or dilutive capital before the B2K closing, compressing equity recovery toward the $6.30 component-low case while fixed claims stay senior. The targeted research loop improved facility-term documentation but did not close line-item debt reconciliation or model punitive-refinance math.

dissent_ledger.issue: Debt waterfall and October 2026 Rialto maturity outcome unresolved
Scarce-asset durability test fails; discount may be rational for a finite wind-down
Base NRV already books B2K high end before entitlement milestones confirm

value_range_per_share.low_source: component valuation schedule (operating and entitlement drivers)
value_range_per_share.high_source: bull scenario payoff if full entitlement success holds 2028 timeline

expected_return_range_pct.note: Low bound reflects component-low annualized path (~3%) and credit-stress framing; base reflects filing NAV realized on schedule. Range is not averaged across methods.

entry_range_per_share.condition: No new capital commitment until Article 78 outcome and B2K site-plan status are confirmed; current price already offers theoretical upside to base NAV but carries unresolved legal and financing checkpoints.

recommendation_rationale: Unanimous watch across three independent groups. The primary liquidation-NAV method shows attractive base math (~33% annualized to $11.79 over ~2.5 years) and even the entitlement-slippage bear (~13.5% to ~$9.00) stays positive, but decision-grade entry is blocked by open Article 78 litigation, unconfirmed B2K site-plan approval with the high-end price already in base NRV, incomplete debt-facility reconciliation, and unquantified dilution or October 2026 refinance stress. This is a monitoring stance, not a sizing or capital decision.

operational_milestones: Article 78 appeal docket outcome and any forced Flowerfield subdivision redesign
Town of Smithtown B2K 49-acre site-plan approval or denial versus contract contingencies
Line-item reconciliation of bank line, Rialto mortgage, and LLYR mortgage to the $10,790,194 loans-payable total with covenant headroom
October 2026 Rialto mortgage maturity: extension, refinance, or default
Quarterly liquidation-basis NAV re-estimation in 10-Q and 10-K filings
B2K contract status: termination, low-end $24.0M close, or high-end $28.74M close
Scan shelf registration and 8-K filings for dilutive equity raises or loan amendments
Cortlandt Manor subdivision progress against the 2027 approval assumption
Star Equity Fund LP standstill expiration (2026-12-31 or 2027 under stated conditions)
valuation_refresh_triggers: Article 78 ruling or Smithtown site-plan decision
Quarterly liquidation-basis NAV revision exceeding $0.50 per share
B2K contract amendment, termination, or closing
Dilutive capital raise or material loan amendment
20% price move without corresponding filing update

price_review_thresholds.action: Re-underwrite downside if catalysts deteriorate; do not add on price alone
Require confirmed entitlement milestones before treating discount as closed
Thesis largely realized; reassess remaining liquidation timeline and distribution path

thesis_break_conditions: Adverse Article 78 ruling or Smithtown denial forces real-estate NRV below the $22.00 per share component low case
B2K deal fails or closes at $24.0M low end with further timeline extension
Failed or punitive October 2026 refinance plus dilutive capital raise cuts per-share recovery below the $6.30 component-low schedule
Another consecutive downward NAV re-estimation cycle without asset-sale proceeds
Liquidation target slips beyond 2028 with rising cost accruals
